segments.title: HMS Zinnia and the Flower-class corvettes
segments.summary: HMS Zinnia is introduced as an early Flower-class corvette-sloop whose service connected both world wars.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke introduces Zinnia rather than the photographed HMS Azalea, the class nameship. He places Zinnia among the early Flower-class corvette-sloops and notes that the ship served through the First and Second World Wars. The brief comparison helps distinguish an individual ship’s career from the wider class name.

evidence.note: The transcript identifies the image as Azalea, then introduces Zinnia as an early Flower-class corvette-sloop serving through both world wars.

segments.title: Zinnia’s Belgian transfer and German capture
segments.summary: After Royal Navy service, Zinnia passed to Belgium, was captured in 1940, and returned to Belgian service after the war.
segments.body: Zinnia remained with the Royal Navy until 1920 before being sold to Belgium. Captured by Germany in 1940, she served as Barbara until the Allies recovered her in 1945. Renamed Breidel in Belgian service, she was ultimately scrapped in 1952, illustrating how a small escort ship could pass through several national careers.

evidence.note: The transcript follows Zinnia from Royal Navy service and sale to Belgium through German capture, postwar return, Belgian renaming, and scrapping.

segments.title: The U-44 encounter off Fastnet Rock
segments.summary: In March 1916, Zinnia fired on U-44 off Fastnet Rock before helping tow the torpedoed Begonia to Queenstown.
segments.body: On 28 March 1916, Zinnia spotted the German submarine U-44 off Fastnet Rock and fired twice, but the submarine escaped by diving. The following day U-44 torpedoed Begonia without sinking her. Zinnia and Snowdrop were sent to assist and towed the damaged vessel to Queenstown, showing the rescue and escort work expected of these small warships.

evidence.note: The transcript describes Zinnia's sighting and shots at U-44, the later torpedoing of Begonia, and the tow to Queenstown with Snowdrop.
